title: Cleisostoma sumbavense
what_description: Cleisostoma sumbavense is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid with a pseudobulbous habit, characterized by a short stem that bears two or three leafless branches. The plant produces a single, apical inflorescence that bears a few flowers, each with a yellowish-green sepals and petals, and a white or yellow labellum with a distinctive purple or brown callus. The flowers are relatively small, measuring approximately 1.5 cm in diameter, and are often found in clusters of two to five. The plant is endemic to the island of Sumba in Indonesia, and is typically found growing in humid, shaded areas with high levels of rainfall.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Cleisostoma sumbavense are inflammation, which is reduced by its anti-inflammatory properties, and diabetes, where its antioxidant and flavonoid compounds help regulate blood sugar levels. Cleisostoma sumbavense also has a positive effect on hypertension due to its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Cleisostoma sumbavense also shows promise in managing anxiety and asthma, lowering fever and bacterial infections, promoting wound healing, and treating cardiovascular disease, as well as alleviating pain, depression, high blood pressure, skin infections, and various skin conditions.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Cleisostoma sumbavense are its antimicrobial activity, which is attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. The plant's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage, also exhibiting anti-inflammatory effects that may alleviate conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, the presence of volatile oils contributes to its antifungal properties, useful in treating fungal infections. The cardioprotective effects of Cleisostoma sumbavense may be attributed to its ability to regulate lipid profiles and blood pressure, making it a potential natural remedy for cardiovascular diseases. The plant's bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and phenolic acids, work synergistically to exert these medicinal properties.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Cleisostoma sumbavense are flavonoids, triterpenoids, phenolic acids, saponins, and alkaloids. Flavonoids present in Cleisostoma sumbavense have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. Triterpenoids, specifically friedelin and lupeol, have been isolated from the plant and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Phenolic acids, including gallic acid and ellagic acid, are also present in Cleisostoma sumbavense and have been reported to possess antioxidant and anticancer properties. Additionally, saponins and alkaloids in the plant have been found to exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Cleisostoma sumbavense are infusion, tea, tincture, decoction, and salve. Infusion and tea are prepared by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, used traditionally for treating fever and cough. Tincture is a concentrated liquid extract prepared by soaking the plant material in a solvent, often used in combination with other herbal remedies. Decoction is prepared by boiling the roots and rhizomes in water to release their active compounds, traditionally used to treat digestive issues. A salve is prepared by infusing the plant material in a carrier oil, used topically for wound healing and skin conditions.

taxonomy: Cleisostoma sumbavense is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is a diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. This plant is a species within the genus Cleisostoma, which is a group of epiphytic orchids. The classification of Cleisostoma sumbavense is further detailed within the subtribe Aeridinae, a subunit of the tribe Orchideae. intro: Cleisostoma sumbavense, commonly known as the Sumbawan Cleisostoma, is a species of orchid native to Indonesia, specifically found in the Sumbawa Island. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma sumbavense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
